npc_system_philosophy: Every NPC is a person with a role, a location tendency, needs of their own (food, shelter, coin). They can die of disease, war, starvation, old age. They can have children slowly replenishing the pool. Population is finite — deaths matter. NPCs work on schedules and do things that make sense for their archetype and profession.
npc_generation_components: Background (how they came to their current situation)
Needs (current hunger, thirst, sleep, health status)
A secret or complication (not always — but enough to feel like real people)

schedule.activity: Rise, morning meal prep, feeding animals
Field work — plowing, planting, or harvesting depending on season
Rest in shade, light meal
Field work continues, lighter tasks in peak summer heat
Return home, animal care
Evening meal, family time, repair of tools and equipment
Sleep

daily_schedules.seasonal_variations: Harvest season — all hands, longer hours. Winter — minimal field work, indoor repair, animal care, near-sedentary

schedule.activity: Rise, breakfast, fire up forge (takes time)
Morning forge work — commission pieces, standard stock
Meal at home or at tavern if nearby
Forge work continues — heavier pieces, apprentice instruction
Close forge, bank coals, clean up
Accounts, material inventory, family time, tavern occasionally
Sleep — smiths sleep hard
Morning muster and inspection (on duty days)
Patrol rotation or gate duty
Meal at garrison or barracks
Continued duty — patrol, watch, escort
End of watch, off-duty time
Tavern on off-days, night watch rotation otherwise

daily_schedules.notes: Drills twice weekly. Rotating watch shifts mean schedule varies by day.

schedule.activity: Inventory check, correspondence, morning market opening
Active trading hours — negotiations, sales
Negotiations continue, factor meetings, supply chain discussions
Accounts, records, planning
Social dining with contacts if prosperous, private accounts if not
Dawn prayer (varies by faith)
Pastoral visits — sick, elderly, recently bereaved in community
At temple for midday observance and open office
Teaching, instruction of acolytes, ritual preparation
Evening service (varies by faith)
Study, correspondence, private prayer
Rise, prep — cleaning, food preparation, barrel check
Open for midday custom — laborers, travelers
Slower period — accounts, supply orders
Busy period — peak trade
Close, final cleanup, accounts
Sleep late
Petitions — common people and minor officials with requests and grievances
Formal meal with household staff, family, or visiting parties
Administration, correspondence, meetings with steward
Social obligations, dining, political entertainment
Private correspondence, planning, occasional travel
Position near temple or market gate for morning traffic
Near food vendors
Market district or well-traveled street
Near tavern entrances
Known shelter spots — specific doorways, specific corners, community of similar

daily_schedules.notes: Beggars have territories, relationships, information networks. They are not background noise.

schedule.activity: Schedule determined by contract and company structure
Tavern, equipment maintenance, looking for work
Rotating watch and patrol schedule per company protocol
Legitimate front business operations if applicable
Meeting contacts, evaluating goods
Primary business — transactions, planning, network maintenance

when_needs_unmet.food: NPCs will spend coin on food before anything else. If no coin, they will seek charitable sources (temple, employer). If nothing, they will steal or beg.
when_needs_unmet.water: NPCs will not travel far from water sources in drought conditions.
when_needs_unmet.shelter: NPCs without fixed shelter will seek it at dusk. They have preferred spots.
when_needs_unmet.coin: NPCs will seek work if coin runs low. Their profession determines what work they seek and where.
